summary: Crisp soundboard capture of an ’89 all‑star show with a blistering second set – Pocky Way → Playing → Rider → Terrapin and a standout Memphis Blues.
review: The Miller‑upgraded soundboard delivers crystal‑clear audio with perfect balance, making every nuance audible. Musically the concert shines especially in the second set where Hey Pocky Way flows into a superb Playing‑Rider‑Terrapin sequence, complemented by an inspired Memphis Blues and memorable solos from Jerry on guitar (and even oboe). Overall the performance is rated among the band’s best of 1989.

ai_rating.rationale: All detailed reviews praise both the musical performance and the recording quality; several give 5‑star ratings, indicating an exceptional show captured with excellent soundboard fidelity.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers repeatedly note the crisp, clear mix and the effective use of FOB/AUD patches; no significant drop‑outs or muddiness reported.

standout_songs: Playing > Know You Rider > Terrapin Station
show_quality.setlist_flow: The set flows tightly, with a high‑energy first half and a second‑set highlight where Pocky Way segues into Playing, Rider (rarely without China Cat) and a massive Terrapin; transitions are seamless and the crowd interaction feels organic.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers praised his guitar work – a beautiful solo on Stagger Lee, flawless tone throughout, and even an oboe cameo adding space.
band_member_comments.Phil: A few notes of technical difficulty with the bass were mentioned, but no specific performance criticism.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keyboards) overcame noted organ/bass glitches, delivering interesting organ work on "Row Jimmy" and supporting the tight jams.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill's drumming got a spotlight during a four‑minute drum break before Terrapin; overall rhythm section praised for energy.
